2605.19936.title: What Are LLMs Doing to Scientific Communication? Measuring Changes in Writing Practices and Reading Experience
2605.19936.paper_summary: Has the style of scientific communication changed due to the growing use of large language models in the writing process? We address this question in the domain of Natural Language Processing by leveraging two data resources we create: a naturalistic corpus of over 37,000 papers from the ACL Anthology (2020-2024); and a synthetic dataset of 3,000 human-written passages and their LLM-generated improvements. We first implement a series of diachronic lexical analyses, showing that both word frequency and usage contexts have changed significantly over time, indicating semantic specialization in some cases and generalization in others. Broadening our perspective, we then model a range of more complex stylistic features and find that LLM-modified texts more frequently contain certain syntactic constructions, more complex and longer words and a lower lexical diversity. Finally, we connect these changes in writing practices to subjective reading experience through a pilot annotation study with 20 domain experts. They overall rate LLM-improved texts as more understandable and exciting, but also express negative qualitative attitudes towards LLMs, highlighting the strongly subjective effect of AI-assisted writing on reading experience.
2605.19936.paper_summary_zh: 科學溝通的風格是否因為大型語言模型在寫作過程中的日益使用而發生了變化？我們在自然語言處理領域通過利用我們創建的兩個數據資源來解決這個問題：一個來自ACL文集（2020-2024）的自然語料庫，包含超過37,000篇論文；以及一個包含3,000篇人類撰寫段落及其LLM生成改進的合成數據集。我們首先實施了一系列歷時性的詞彙分析，顯示出單詞頻率和使用語境隨時間顯著變化，表明在某些情況下語意專門化，而在其他情況下則是一般化。擴大我們的視角後，我們建模了一系列更複雜的風格特徵，發現LLM修改的文本更頻繁地包含某些句法結構，更複雜和更長的單詞，以及較低的詞彙多樣性。最後，我們通過一項與20位領域專家的初步標註研究將這些寫作實踐的變化與主觀閱讀體驗聯繫起來。他們普遍評價LLM改進的文本更易於理解和更具吸引力，但也表達了對LLM的負面質性態度，突顯了AI輔助寫作對閱讀體驗的強烈主觀影響。
2605.19936.author: Filip Miletić et.al.
